patch.value: Lock contention auto-retry: bounded jittered wait before lock_conflict so parallel agent mutations self-heal

Parallel mutations on the SAME item hard-fail immediately: acquireLock (src/core/lock/lock.ts) throws lock_conflict (exit 4) the moment a live lock exists — no wait, no retry. Empirical: 12 parallel `pm update` on one item → 5 succeed, 7 lock_conflict, even though each lock is held only for milliseconds. Agents firing parallel tool calls (or multiple agent sessions/programmatic spawns) must hand-roll retry loops. Fix: bounded jittered retry inside acquireLock before throwing — e.g. settings `locks.wait_ms` (default ~3000, 0 = today's fail-fast) + `PM_LOCK_WAIT_MS` env override + `--lock-wait-ms` flag; poll with jittered backoff (e.g. 25→50→100→200ms) until acquired or budget exhausted. Also add `retry_after_ms` hint to the structured lock_conflict error payload so callers that still time out know to retry. Integrity is already perfect under contention (no corruption, no lock debris, history consistent) — this is purely availability/UX. Note: a single pm-mcp server already serializes via SerialQueue (pm-3puw), so this mainly hits parallel CLI invocations and multiple concurrent MCP/agent sessions on one workspace.

author: claude-code-agent

patch.value: Parallel mutations on the SAME item still surface lock_conflict too quickly under burst contention: acquireLock (src/core/lock/lock.ts) already retries EEXIST a few times, but it does not wait with a configurable jittered budget before failing. Empirical: 12 parallel `pm update` on one item -> 5 succeed, 7 lock_conflict, even though each lock is held only for milliseconds. Agents firing parallel tool calls (or multiple agent sessions/programmatic spawns) still need hand-rolled retry loops. Fix: bounded jittered wait inside acquireLock before throwing lock_conflict - e.g. settings `locks.wait_ms` (default ~3000, 0 = today's fail-fast) + `PM_LOCK_WAIT_MS` env override + `--lock-wait-ms` flag; poll with jittered backoff (e.g. 25->50->100->200ms) until acquired or budget exhausted. If the wait budget exhausts, include a `retry_after_ms` hint in the structured lock_conflict payload so callers know when to retry. Integrity is already perfect under contention (no corruption, no lock debris, history consistent) - this is purely availability/UX. Note: a single pm-mcp server already serializes via SerialQueue (pm-3puw), so this mainly hits parallel CLI invocations and multiple concurrent MCP/agent sessions on one workspace.

message: Review wording fix: clarify existing EEXIST retries and missing jittered wait budget
